concerns.text: FR-002 explicitly mandates a decision logic using both Shapiro-Wilk and Levene's tests to select between T-Test and Wilcoxon. Tasks T022 and T023 implement only Shapiro-Wilk and Repeated Measures ANOVA, leaving the Levene's test requirement and the T-Test/Wilcoxon fallback path unimplemented. While the Plan notes a conflict with the Constitution, the Task list does not reflect the Plan's resolution (ANOVA) as a formal spec change, creating a semantic gap where the FR is effectively weakened without a documented override.

FR-002 and US-002 list 'explanation_engagement_time' as a metric requiring statistical significance testing. Task T023 explicitly excludes this metric from inferential testing. The Plan justifies this as 'tautological' (comparing to zero), but the Task list implements this exclusion without a corresponding update to the FR or a documented 'staged simplification' marker, constituting a silent relaxation of the requirement to test all listed metrics.

User Story 0 requires the system to integrate specific XAI techniques (e.g., SHAP, LIME) as configurable options. Task T013 hardcodes a 'deterministic, rule-based simulation' mapping task difficulty to heatmaps. This removes the ability to toggle between different real XAI algorithms as originally specified in the FR/US, weakening the requirement to 'integrate specific XAI techniques' into a synthetic simulation without an explicit spec amendment.

EC-001 requires the system to 'report the actual N achieved and flag the power limitation' if recruitment targets are not met for a specific subgroup. Task T030 only generates a report if N < 30 (aggregate). It fails to implement the logic for flagging power limitations when the aggregate target is met but subgroups are underpowered, as noted in the Plan's scope adjustment. This drops a specific acceptance criterion from the Edge Cases.
